title: Dynamic Workflows
description: Define JavaScript dynamic workflows and look up Factory Session API interface and configuration for you-agent-factory.
openingSummary: A dynamic workflow is a JavaScript (JAVASCRIPT) orchestrator-backed factory. The live run is a Factory Session — the same durable runtime object used for every live orchestration — not a separate DynamicWorkflowRun type.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: A dynamic workflow is a JavaScript (JAVASCRIPT) orchestrator-backed factory in you-agent-factory. Customers author JavaScript or TypeScript orchestrator source, then validate, start, and inspect the live run as a Factory Session. This documentation defines that idea in isolation, names the session nouns a live run depends on, and then documents the callable API interface and configuration knobs for source resolution and Model Context Protocol (MCP) serve modes.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: A Factory is the authored orchestration definition a live session loads. A Factory Orchestrator (FactoryOrchestrator) is the orchestrator identity on that factory — Petri (PETRI) or JavaScript (JAVASCRIPT). Dynamic workflow is shorthand for the JavaScript kind. A Factory Session (FactorySession) is the canonical runtime instance for one live orchestration; a dynamic-workflow run is still that Factory Session with JavaScript orchestrator kind, not a separate DynamicWorkflowRun noun. A Dispatch is a shared execution request the session sends. A Factory Artifact (FactoryArtifact) is a session-owned durable output such as a result, finding, log, or checkpoint reference. A Factory Event (FactoryEvent) is a durable event-stream record for session lifecycle, dispatch lifecycle, progress, and artifacts.

apiInterface.title: API Interface
apiInterface.body: The API interface for dynamic workflows covers CLI workflow commands for JavaScript Factory Session work, Model Context Protocol (MCP) Factory Session tools with compatibility aliases, and durable session inspection for list, show, events, and preview style lookups.

configuration.title: Configuration
configuration.body: Configuration for dynamic workflows covers workflow source resolution from a project root, source kinds such as WORKFLOW_NAME and INLINE_WORKFLOW, optional args and policy overrides, and Model Context Protocol (MCP) serve backing modes for fixture-backed versus runtime-backed hosts.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Start from the definition and key concepts to learn what a dynamic workflow is and which Factory Session nouns it uses. Open the API interface section when you need callable CLI, Model Context Protocol (MCP), or session inspection surfaces. Open the configuration section when you need source-resolution or MCP serve-mode knobs.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This documentation is a web reference for JavaScript dynamic-workflow API interface and configuration. It is not the Cursor first-loop guide, not the full Model Context Protocol (MCP) host catalog deep dive beyond dynamic-workflow needs, and not the Petri factory.json topology overview owned by the configuration documentation page.
